titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/103

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=716 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:19:38.19818+00 | translation moa=717 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:19:33.840398+00

div.p:
. W. dei Gratia Rex Scottorum . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitis . Baronibus . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris .
et Omnibus probis Hominibus Tocius Terre Sue . Clericis et laicis . Salutem . Sciant presentes
et futuri . me Concessisse . et Hac Carta mea Confirmasse . amicabilem compositionem factam .
apud Seleschr’ . in presentia mea . et in plena curia mea . ex una parte inter Comitem . Patricium . de Dunbar’ . consentitiente . Patricio . filio . et herede eius . et domum de Melros . ex altera parte .
super controuersia que uertebatur inter eos de pastura uersus occidentem a ledre . Quare uolo et
firmiter precipio . quatinus predicta compositio inter eos inde facta inuiolabiliter et inconcusse te
neatur  sicut Cyrographum inter eos inde factum  testatur . saluo seruicio meo . Testibus . B . episcopo Mora
uiensi . Rad’ . episcopo de Duhn’ . Willelmo . et Oliuer’ . Capellanis meis . Roberto de london’ . filio . meo . DauiD . de lindes’ . et Geruasio . Auenel . Iustic’ . Willelmo de Bosch’ . et Hugone . de sigill’ . Clericis meis . Rogero .
de mortemar . Adam . de Kingor . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Alexandro . de Sint’ . Apud Selesch’ . vi . die
Nouembris .

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, the friendly agreement made at Selkirk in my presence and in my full court, on the one side between Earl Patrick of Dunbar, with his son and heir, Patrick, consenting, and the house of Melrose on the other side, about the disagreement which arose between them concerning the pasture towards the west of Leader. Wherefore I will and firmly instruct that the said agreement between them made thereupon be inviolably and constantly held fast, just as the cyrograph made between them bears witness. Saving my service. As witnesses, Brice bishop of Moray, Ralph bishop of Down, William and Oliver my chaplains, Robert of London my son, David of Lindsay and Gervase Avenel, justices, William del Bois and Hugh de Sigillo my clerks, Roger de Mortimer, Adam of Kinghorn, John of Maxwell, Alexander of Synton. At Selkirk on the 6th day of November.
